articles.title: INSTAGRAM'S NEW ‘INSTANTS' FEATURE COMBINES ELEMENTS FROM SNAPCHAT AND BEREAL
articles.summary: Instagram is globally launching Instants, a new disappearing photo feature inspired by Snapchat and BeReal that focuses on authentic sharing among close friends and mutual followers. Users can only capture unedited photos with Instagram's in-app camera, with each Instant viewable once for 24 hours before disappearing. The feature marks Meta's push to bring Instagram back toward more casual, private interactions, though it arrives as many users already rely on Stories for quick updates.

articles.title: APPLE'S LATEST ACQUISITION FURTHER CONFIRMS A NEW PRIORITY FOR THE COMPANY
articles.summary: Apple has quietly acquired Patchflyer, the company behind the popular browser-based color grading app Color.io, further signaling that its new Creator Studio ecosystem is a major priority. The acquisition follows Apple's recent purchases of Pixelmator and MotionVFX, suggesting the company is actively building a stronger suite of professional tools for video creators, photographers, and filmmakers across apps like Final Cut Pro and potentially even the iPhone camera system.

articles.title: AMAZON LAUNCHES AN AI SHOPPING ASSISTANT FOR THE SEARCH BAR, POWERED BY ALEXA+
articles.summary: Amazon has launched Alexa for Shopping, a new AI-powered assistant integrated into the Amazon search bar and powered by Alexa+. Replacing Rufus, it offers personalized recommendations, shopping guides, price tracking, recurring orders, and even the ability to purchase products from other online retailers using Amazon's “Buy for Me” feature. Available across mobile, desktop, and Echo Show devices, the assistant is designed to learn customers' habits over time and automate parts of the shopping experience as Amazon continues expanding its AI-powered commerce tools.

articles.title: DESIGNERS ARE A RARE BREED
articles.summary: Designers represent only 0.25% of the US workforce, making them extremely rare, with most companies handling design poorly or not at all. New AI design tools like Claude Design aren't replacing professional designers but rather raising the design quality floor for businesses that never would have hired designers. For actual designers, this creates more leverage and opportunity since raising the floor doesn't limit their ceiling - their expertise and taste remain irreplaceable advantages.

articles.title: HOW TO MAKE CLAUDE CODE FOLLOW YOUR DESIGN SYSTEM IN FIGMA
articles.summary: Claude Code writes directly to Figma via Figma MCP but ignores design system governance, using raw hex values and improvised components instead of tokens and library instances. To fix this, the author built four Claude Code Skills — Preflight, Reference Interpreter, Component Rules, and Style Binding — that enforce design system compliance at every layer before and during canvas construction. The same tool can then shift roles: acting as a strict design assistant when iteration matters, or a fast prototyper when speed takes priority.

articles.title: APPLE'S FOLDING IPHONE SUDDENLY DOESN'T SOUND SO 'ULTRA'
articles.summary: Rumors suggest Apple's upcoming folding iPhone — possibly branded as the “iPhone Ultra” rather than “iPhone Fold” — may drop Face ID in favor of Touch ID to achieve a thinner design. According to reports, the device could be too slim to fit Apple's current Face ID hardware, so fingerprint authentication may return via the power button, similar to the iPad Air. While some may see this as a downgrade for a premium device expected to cost around $2,000, the tradeoff could allow Apple to create a more compact foldable phone with a large display that remains small and portable when closed.

articles.title: TAILGRIDS UI
articles.summary: TailGrids UI is a complete React component library built with Tailwind CSS that offers 600+ free and pro components, blocks, and templates for faster web development.

articles.title: VIRALITY PREDICTOR
articles.summary: Upload a short clip and get a modeled read on its hook strength, attention curve, and viral potential.

articles.title: EXTRACT STYLES FROM ANY WEBSITE — MIROMIRO
articles.summary: MiroMiro is a Chrome extension that allows users to extract CSS, Tailwind code, design tokens, images, and Lottie animations from any website with one click.

articles.title: HOW TO CREATE WEBSITES WITH GREAT UX DESIGNS: PRINCIPLES AND EXAMPLES
articles.summary: Great UX websites prioritize user-first design, usability, consistency, accessibility, visual hierarchy, and contextual information to help visitors navigate smoothly and complete desired actions. Quality user experiences make visitors more likely to remember and return to a brand, and build positive associations with it, ultimately driving conversions and long-term growth. The article explores core UX design principles and showcases seven websites that demonstrate effective user experience design in practice.

articles.title: AGENTIC DESIGN SYSTEM - FROM CHATBOT TO ORCHESTRATION
articles.summary: Design systems are evolving from passive component libraries into operational infrastructure that AI agents can read, interpret, and act upon — with human oversight. This shift requires treating components as contracts that encode not just structure, but intent, constraints, accessibility rules, and escalation logic. Teams that add semantic metadata to tokens and build governance frameworks will be better positioned than those still optimizing for faster component generation.

articles.title: HOW TO DO UX RESEARCH IN THE AGE OF AI
articles.summary: AI can identify trends and summarize information, but it still struggles to understand the complexity of real human behavior in specific environments. Contextual research — observing people in their homes, noticing physical habits, cultural differences, emotional reactions, and communication patterns — reveals insights that data alone often misses. Whether studying accessibility needs, older adults' sense of familiarity, or how people naturally interact with devices, the most valuable findings come from human interpretation, empathy, and observation rather than pattern recognition alone.

articles.title: 1,000 RECORD COVERS: THE BOOK THAT PROVES ALBUM ART IS HIGH ART
articles.summary: "1,000 Record Covers" by Michael Ochs presents 574 pages of album art from the 1960s–1990s as a compelling argument that album cover design deserves recognition as high art.

articles.title: WHAT IS URBAN DENSITY DESIGN? A CLEAR GUIDE TO HOW CITIES GET BUILT DENSER
articles.summary: Urban density design is the practice of planning cities to fit more people, activities, and uses into a given area while maintaining livability and quality public spaces.
